msg: Control flow of non-void function ends here!
reasoning: Lines 90-93: 'static int setup_cpu_cache()' is declared with return type int but contains no return statement — the function simply calls kmalloc_node(GFP_KERNEL) and falls off the end without returning a value. This is a genuine missing-return control_flow bug; in C, falling off the end of a non-void, non-main function without a return statement is undefined behavior. The function is defined inside #ifdef CONFIG_SLAB (line 49), so the presence condition def(CONFIG_SLAB) is correctly constrained. The vbdb entry for eb91f1d0a53 documents the IRQ-disabled + GFP_KERNEL (sleepable) allocation bug where assert(!irqs_disabled) fails under CONFIG_LOCKDEP&&CONFIG_TRACE_IRQFLAGS&&CONFIG_PROVE_LOCKING&&CONFIG_SLAB — a different (runtime assertion) bug. The missing return in setup_cpu_cache is a new finding. New true positive.
